rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections in the input AADL model
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are properly scoped and do not reference out-of-scope identifiers
Avoid placing assumptions or guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation scopes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type scopes when concrete bounds are available
Write output dependency contracts in component type scopes using visible inputs and outputs
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid placing assumptions or guarantees in scopes where the referenced ports are not visible
Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in locations where they would create scope conflicts or ambiguity
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
